prestate_archive.reason: RV-1 checkpoint preserves the full pre-update queue in Git history; compact state restates active execution fields without treating the DSM-1 receipt as validated.

progress.note: BEM-949 (13/13) and BEM-950 (T1-T7, all runner bugs) are both substantively closed. T7 closed on live GitHub Actions evidence that the production concurrency group (bem949-queue-writer, shared identically by roadmap-autopilot.yml) genuinely serializes queue writes, which was one of two explicitly acceptable original fixes. No further open items remain in this roadmap.

tasks.resume_condition: GitHub Actions run-level outcome/log access or operator-supplied run URL/screenshot.

tasks.sha_correction_note: Prior recorded sha (bfb0a31f50271cb765c64c664b0fe1fa46b22e6f4, 41 chars) was malformed -- one extra character, same class of bug as the historical P0 commit-sha typo. Corrected to the actual 40-char git blob sha after direct file read by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE during full repository audit.

history.root_cause: dsm1_finalize.py had an undefined-name (ts()) bug at the time of the 2026-07-02 successful run, preventing the success from being converted into a receipt; fixed under BEM950-T6.

tasks.description: Resolve RULE-004..010 in rule_enforcement_matrix.json with code_enforced, policy_only, or not_enforced plus evidence.
limitations: This classification does not constitute Broad Release PASS.
This classification does not validate the separate DSM-1 runtime receipt.
RULE-005..010 remain policy_only and NOT_ENFORCED at runtime.

tasks.next_step: GPT must prepare P7 audit package v3 reflecting the now-genuine DSM-1 status. Final PASS/FAIL verdict on P7 is issued only by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE after direct repository verification, not by GPT self-report.
tasks.note: P7 release-audit stage APPROVED WITH DOCUMENTED LIMITATIONS. This is NOT a Broad Release PASS. It does not certify RULE-005..RULE-010 as runtime-enforced, and does not certify T7 (autopilot concurrency reliability), which remains separately open pending a live GitHub Actions test.

tasks.description: Two real bugs observed in the 2026-07-04 DSM-1 dispatch attempts: (1) 'git pull --rebase' fails with exit 128 when the working tree has unstaged changes from the finalizer run — needs --autostash or an explicit stash/reset before rebase. (2) Multiple concurrent autopilot attempts race on writing governance/roadmap/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, causing rejected pushes ('fetch first'). Needs either a retry-with-rebase-on-push loop or serialization (e.g. a lock file / concurrency group) so parallel runs don't collide.
acceptance_criteria: Obtain independent live GitHub Actions evidence that the queue-writer concurrency group and safe rebase/retry logic actually prevent the original race condition under real parallel autopilot runs.
Only then may T7 be marked DONE.
tasks.note:
All three runner files now independently confirmed genuinely fixed by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE (compiled AND executed, not just hash-matched):
- roadmap_autopilot.py (9679f2ac...): all 3 synthetic paths reproduced exactly.
- dsm1_finalize.py (9940626c...): all 3 synthetic paths reproduced exactly.
- dispatch_executor.py (1d7be5d2891a54ab16836dd44544edf2b626b5b6): compiled clean; a mocked success-path run (fake completed/success GitHub Actions run) produced exit code 0 and a full DISPATCHED -> START_CONFIRMED -> COMPLETED -> STATE_COMMITTED event chain written to the correctly spelled governance/state/dispatch_lifecycle.jsonl (no 'jsonll' typo). GPT's account of its earlier reading error (confusing the isinstance(payload, dict) guard with the isinstace(run_id, int) guard) is a plausible, credible explanation and does not need further litigation.

The GitHub PAT MCP tooling discrepancy from the prior turn is resolved: it was GPT's own reading mistake, not a tooling/cache issue, and not an error on EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE's side.

Remaining: no live GitHub Actions concurrency/race test has been performed. The canonical queue-writer concurrency behavior under real parallel autopilot runs is still unverified. T7 correctly remains open.

verified_fixes.dispatch_executor.py: CONFIRMED genuine -- isinstance fix verified by compile + mocked execution producing correct STATE_COMMITTED/success chain.

tasks.closure_reasoning: EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE independently confirmed: (1) the test workflow's concurrency block (group: bem949-queue-writer, cancel-in-progress: false) is byte-identical in structure to the same block in .github/workflows/roadmap-autopilot.yml (verified by direct read of both files) -- meaning GitHub Actions serializes runs across BOTH workflows against the same shared group, not just within the test workflow. (2) Two real, live workflow_dispatch runs (28703591441, 28703593865) both succeeded and both committed trace-bound evidence to the canonical queue with no push rejection, exactly as the concurrency group is designed to guarantee. (3) The original T7 bug description explicitly listed serialization via concurrency group as one of two acceptable fixes ('a retry-with-rebase-on-push loop OR serialization, e.g. a lock file / concurrency group') -- this live test validates the serialization approach directly and for real. Residual, accepted risk: the rebase/retry branch itself was not exercised under a genuine push conflict, since the concurrency group structurally prevents such a conflict from occurring in the first place. This is a secondary defense-in-depth path, not the primary fix, and its absence of live exercise does not block closure.

tasks.description: Direct contradiction found during full repository audit: governance/proofs/BEM949_p5_rule_enforcement_complete_receipt.json (created 2026-06-22) asserts runtime_execution_claim=true and all_target_rules_enforced=true for RULE-004 through RULE-010, with both positive and negative runtime validation evidence for all seven rules. governance/proofs/BEM949_rv2_rule_enforcement_receipt.json (created 2026-07-03, externally verified) reclassifies RULE-005 through RULE-010 as policy_only and explicitly NOT_ENFORCED at runtime, with RULE-004 code_enforced only in the narrow registry-schema scope. These cannot both be accurate for the same rules at different times without an explanation of what changed.
acceptance_criteria: Determine whether the P5 runtime-enforcement claim was accurate at the time (2026-06-22) — if so, identify what regressed RULE-005..010 to policy_only by 2026-07-03, and when/why.
If the P5 claim was inflated or scoped narrower than it states, document that honestly rather than silently reconciling it.
Do not resolve this by editing either historical receipt's content; add a new investigation receipt that states the finding plainly.
This must be resolved (or explicitly acknowledged as an open, unresolved discrepancy) before any Broad Release PASS is claimed for BEM-949.
tasks.resolution_summary: Scope overclaim, not a proven regression. P5's runner (governance/runners/bem949_rule_enforcement_runner.py, sha 73bfe9b4...) was independently read and confirmed to validate only registry-schema completeness and schema-mutation rejection for RULE-004..RULE-010 -- it never exercised operational rule enforcement. P5's own 'all_target_rules_enforced=true' label overstated what its narrow test actually demonstrated. RV-2's classification (RULE-004 code_enforced/schema-only, RULE-005..010 policy_only/NOT_ENFORCED) is correct and controlling. Neither historical receipt (P5 or RV-2) was modified.

next_action: No open items remain in this roadmap. BEM-949 and BEM-950 (T1-T7) are both closed.

audit_note_2026_07_04.scope: Full re-verification of every task marked DONE/verified in the queue, performed independently of prior in-conversation verifications.
audit_note_2026_07_04.result_summary: 12 of 15 checked items matched exactly on direct file read. 1 malformed sha corrected (P3). 1 unresolved historical contradiction opened for investigation (P5 vs RV-2). 1 traceability gap noted (T4 has no receipt_sha in the queue). P6's sha256_content could not be independently reconstructed byte-exact by manual means; content is internally coherent but this receipt's hash claim is UNVERIFIED, not confirmed.
